{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Officer, Quality Control",
  "description": "Stanbic IBTC Bank is a leading African banking group focused on emerging markets globally. It has been a mainstay of South Africa&#39;s financial system for 150 years, and now spans 16 countries across the African continent.

Standard Bank is a firm believer in technical innovation, to help us guarantee exceptional client service and leading edge financial solutions. Our growing global success reflects our commitment to the latest solutions, the best people, and a uniquely flexible and vibrant working culture. To help us drive our success into the future, we are looking for resourceful individuals to join our dedicated team at our offices.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Officer, Quality Control

Job Ref ID: 80450647A-0001
Job Location:&nbsp;Ilupeju Industrial Avenue, Lagos

Job Description


	To ensure accurate and timely reconciliation of all cheques and cards delivered by the vendor against the production reports sent in by card services and those transmitted to vendors via SFTP.
	Prepare the card dispatch schedule to track the number cheques and cards sent to each branch.
	Ensure that all cheques and cards are forwarded to the UPS mail room within the agreed SLA.
	Adequate record keeping of all production reports sent to the team for processing.
	Daily call over of the delivery schedule against the production reports.


Responsibilties


	Drive innovative ideas and implementation that will influence reduction in operating cost.
	Strategic assists to develop and continuously improves the Cheques and cards area in collaboration with the Team leader, Quality Control with a view of ensuring and effective functioning and high level of output within the Quality Control area.
	Customer and Business Partner Relationship Management Cost Management. Drives within own team initiatives to reduce cost and ensures that budgets for the Account Maintenance area are met. Ensures that throughput is maximized throughout the Ledger area by noting processes that could be improved applying the principles of Lean manufacturing and Six Sigma.
	Resource utilization and maintain a cooperative supportive working relationship with other team members.&nbsp;
	Respond to email queries relating to cheques and cards dispatch status timely. Manage branches&rsquo; expectation by informing them of possible delays due to late delivery of cheques and cards by the vendor.


Qualifications


	BSc Degree in Finance and Accounting.


Experience:


	1 - 2 years of risk identification and assessment skills experience
	Experience in the operations area, of quality assurance and service monitoring is preferable.&nbsp;


Behavioural Competencies:


	Developing Expertise
	Following Procedures
	Interacting with People
	Team Working
	Upholding Standards.


Technical Competencies:


	Coaching and Mentoring
	Continuous Improvement
	Data Management (Administration)
	Project Management (Project Mgmt)
	Quality Control
	Quality Management.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-23",
  "validThrough": "2026-04-24",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Stanbic IBTC Bank"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Lagos",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },
  "identifier": {
    "@type": "PropertyValue",
    "name": "",
    "value": "196320"
  },
  "baseSalary": {
    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",
    "currency": "NGN",
    "value": {
      "@type": "QuantitativeValue",
      "minValue": 0,
      "maxValue": 0,
      "unitText": "MONTH"
    }
  }
}



Officer, Quality Control Job at Stanbic IBTC Bank




        
    

        
        Stanbic IBTC Bank is a leading African banking group focused on emerging markets globally. It has been a mainstay of South Africa's financial system for 150 years, and now spans 16 countries across the African continent.

Standard Bank is a firm believer in technical innovation, to help us guarantee exceptional client service and leading edge financial solutions. Our growing global success reflects our commitment to the latest solutions, the best people, and a uniquely flexible and vibrant working culture. To help us drive our success into the future, we are looking for resourceful individuals to join our dedicated team at our offices.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Officer, Quality Control
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Ref ID: 80450647A-0001
Job Location: Ilupeju Industrial Avenue, Lagos

Job Description


	To ensure accurate and timely reconciliation of all cheques and cards delivered by the vendor against the production reports sent in by card services and those transmitted to vendors via SFTP.
	Prepare the card dispatch schedule to track the number cheques and cards sent to each branch.
	Ensure that all cheques and cards are forwarded to the UPS mail room within the agreed SLA.
	Adequate record keeping of all production reports sent to the team for processing.
	Daily call over of the delivery schedule against the production reports.


Responsibilties


	Drive innovative ideas and implementation that will influence reduction in operating cost.
	Strategic assists to develop and continuously improves the Cheques and cards area in collaboration with the Team leader, Quality Control with a view of ensuring and effective functioning and high level of output within the Quality Control area.
	Customer and Business Partner Relationship Management Cost Management. Drives within own team initiatives to reduce cost and ensures that budgets for the Account Maintenance area are met. Ensures that throughput is maximized throughout the Ledger area by noting processes that could be improved applying the principles of Lean manufacturing and Six Sigma.
	Resource utilization and maintain a cooperative supportive working relationship with other team members. 
	Respond to email queries relating to cheques and cards dispatch status timely. Manage branches’ expectation by informing them of possible delays due to late delivery of cheques and cards by the vendor.


Qualifications


	BSc Degree in Finance and Accounting.


Experience:


	1 - 2 years of risk identification and assessment skills experience
	Experience in the operations area, of quality assurance and service monitoring is preferable. 


Behavioural Competencies:


	Developing Expertise
	Following Procedures
	Interacting with People
	Team Working
	Upholding Standards.


Technical Competencies:


	Coaching and Mentoring
	Continuous Improvement
	Data Management (Administration)
	Project Management (Project Mgmt)
	Quality Control
	Quality Management.
    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Head of Finance & Accounts",
  "description": "RevoHealth (RVH) is a Nigerian Health Maintenance Organisation (HMO) established as a transformative health financing initiative committed to redefining access to healthcare across Africa. We are on a mission to drastically reduce out-of-pocket healthcare payments, eliminate financial barriers, and deploy technology to overcome physical access challenges, making healthcare truly accessible to underserved populations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Finance &amp; Accounts

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Purpose


	Manage, control and ensure that company accounting activities and procedures conform to generally accepted accounting principles.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement the company&rsquo;s financial strategy in alignment with overall business objectives
	Manage financial planning, budgeting, and forecasting.
	Develop financial policies that ensure compliance and integrity.
	Monitor cash flow, accounts, and payroll
	Prepare transparent, insightful financial reports for stakeholders.


Person Specification


	Education and Skills: Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Finance, Accounting, or related fields
	Experience: 8+ years in finance, preferably in healthcare or HMO.
	Proficient in financial reporting, budgeting, and accounting software
	Qualities: Ethical, analytical, detail-oriented, with a strategic perspective.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-23",
  "validThrough": "2026-04-21",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "RevoHealth (RVH)"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Abuja",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },

  },

    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",

    }

}



Head of Finance & Accounts Job at RevoHealth (RVH)




        
    

        
        RevoHealth (RVH) is a Nigerian Health Maintenance Organisation (HMO) established as a transformative health financing initiative committed to redefining access to healthcare across Africa. We are on a mission to drastically reduce out-of-pocket healthcare payments, eliminate financial barriers, and deploy technology to overcome physical access challenges, making healthcare truly accessible to underserved populations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Finance & Accounts

Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Purpose
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Manage, control and ensure that company accounting activities and procedures conform to generally accepted accounting principles.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement the company’s financial strategy in alignment with overall business objectives
	Manage financial planning, budgeting, and forecasting.
	Develop financial policies that ensure compliance and integrity.
	Monitor cash flow, accounts, and payroll
	Prepare transparent, insightful financial reports for stakeholders.


Person Specification


	Education and Skills: Bachelor’s Degree in Finance, Accounting, or related fields
	Experience: 8+ years in finance, preferably in healthcare or HMO.
	Proficient in financial reporting, budgeting, and accounting software
	Qualities: Ethical, analytical, detail-oriented, with a strategic perspective.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

Junior Interior Designer / Project Associate (Contract) Job at Arabesque Living




        
    

        
        Arabesque Living is an interir design studio creating timeless and multifunctional spaces that enhance quality living. We focus on both commercial and residential interior design.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Junior Interior Designer / Project Associate (Contract)
Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Contract; Hybrid (3 days onsite)

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Assist in developing interior design concepts and technical drawings
	Prepare moodboards, presentations and material selections
	Manage project timelines and coordinate with contractors
	Conduct site visits and ensure projects are executed according to the design plans, and provide project updates.


Requirements


	Bachelors Degree in Interior Design, Architecture or related fields
	2-3 years experience in interior design or architecture
	Proficiency in AutoCAD, SketchUp, and design presentation tools
	Willing to undergo a minimum of 3 months of contract
	Good understanding of materials, finishes and space planning
	Good project coordination and communication skills.


Salary
N200,000 per month. 

Method of Application 
Intrested and qualified candidates should send their CV and/or Portfolio to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only qualified residents currently residing within Lagos state will be contacted.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Officer",
  "description": "TalentNova HR - Our client is a fast?growing trading company focused on marketing and distributing petroleum products.

Job Position: Business Development Officer
Job Location:&nbsp;Ojodu Berger, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Sector:&nbsp;Downstream Oil and Gas Sector
Industry: Downstream Oil and Gas (marketing and trading of petroleum products to industrial, commercial, and retail end users)

Role Overview


	We provide fuel solutions to industrial, commercial, and retail customers while building long?term commercial partnerships.
	We are hiring a Business Development Officer to grow our customer base and revenue by identifying opportunities, following up with customers, marketing and selling petroleum products, managing a customer portfolio, and ensuring high levels of customer satisfaction.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify opportunities across industrial, commercial, and retail downstream markets.
	Follow up with customers to convert leads and maintain strong relationships.
	Market and sell petroleum products (diesel, AGO, PMS, lubricants, petrochemicals etc.)
	Manage customer portfolio to maximize retention, growth, and revenue.
	Ensure customer satisfaction through timely service, issue resolution, and proactive account management.
	Prepare sales reports and provide market feedback to support pricing and supply decisions.
	Meet periodic targets


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Business Admin, Marketing or related field.
	Minimum of five years proven work experience in sales, business development, or account management, preferably in downstream oil and gas, fuel supply, or petroleum trading.
	Strong negotiation, communication, and relationship?building skills.
	Target driven, self?starter, and able to manage multiple accounts.
	Familiarity with industrial fuel supply chains, product specifications, and logistics is an advantage.
	Valid driver&rsquo;s license and willingness to travel to client sites.


Salary
N200,000 - N300,000 per month.&nbsp;

What We Offer:


	Competitive salary and performance?based incentives.
	Clear career progression in a fast?paced sector.
	Supportive professional environment and on?the?job training.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and cover letter to:&nbsp;hr@neotericoilandgas.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note:&nbsp;Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.",

  },

Business Development Officer Job at TalentNova HR




        
    

        
        TalentNova HR - Our client is a fast?growing trading company focused on marketing and distributing petroleum products.

Job Position: Business Development Officer
Job Location: Ojodu Berger, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Sector: Downstream Oil and Gas Sector
Industry: Downstream Oil and Gas (marketing and trading of petroleum products to industrial, commercial, and retail end users)

Role Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We provide fuel solutions to industrial, commercial, and retail customers while building long?term commercial partnerships.
	We are hiring a Business Development Officer to grow our customer base and revenue by identifying opportunities, following up with customers, marketing and selling petroleum products, managing a customer portfolio, and ensuring high levels of customer satisfaction.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify opportunities across industrial, commercial, and retail downstream markets.
	Follow up with customers to convert leads and maintain strong relationships.
	Market and sell petroleum products (diesel, AGO, PMS, lubricants, petrochemicals etc.)
	Manage customer portfolio to maximize retention, growth, and revenue.
	Ensure customer satisfaction through timely service, issue resolution, and proactive account management.
	Prepare sales reports and provide market feedback to support pricing and supply decisions.
	Meet periodic targets


Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Business Admin, Marketing or related field.
	Minimum of five years proven work experience in sales, business development, or account management, preferably in downstream oil and gas, fuel supply, or petroleum trading.
	Strong negotiation, communication, and relationship?building skills.
	Target driven, self?starter, and able to manage multiple accounts.
	Familiarity with industrial fuel supply chains, product specifications, and logistics is an advantage.
	Valid driver’s license and willingness to travel to client sites.


Salary
N200,000 - N300,000 per month. 

What We Offer:


	Competitive salary and performance?based incentives.
	Clear career progression in a fast?paced sector.
	Supportive professional environment and on?the?job training.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and cover letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Bank and Tax Accountant",
  "description": "MarbleClear Limited, a human Resource Consulting firm, is&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Bank and Tax Accountant

Job Location:&nbsp;Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
Bank Reconciliation:


	Daily Transactions: Review and record daily bank transactions, including deposits, withdrawals, transfers, and journal entries.
	Reconciliation: Perform bank reconciliations, including matching bank charges and interest with internal records.
	Compliance: Ensure compliance with banking regulations and internal control policies.
	Preparing the documents required to request bank credit, following up on the credit activation after issuance and reviewing its balances (for letters of credit &amp; letters of guarantee/ loans).
	Following-up long-term loan balances, debit interest, loan repayment installments, bank account reconciliations, and preparation of journal accounting entries for loans and accrued interest.
	Following up, controlling and reviewing bank charges and commissions and their conformity with the contracts concluded, analysis them and reporting to the direct manager
	review the bank charges and commissions that are deducted from the company&#39;s current account and must review their value according to the contract with the bank
	Initiate and Register e-Form M: Complete and register electronic Form M on the&nbsp;Nigeria Single Window Trade Portal&nbsp;for all importations
	Revalue open accounts payable (AP) and accounts receivable (AR) in foreign currencies at month-end.
	Calculate and record unrealized exchange gains or losses in the General Ledger.


Tax:


	Monthly provisions for minimum tax and additional PAYE
	Monthly compliance on VAT, WHT, PAYE, Pension, NSITF and Union and coop deductions ensure correct deductions and calculations have been inputted into the system.
	Review all calculations, prepare schedule for payment and payment instruction where applicable, file monthly returns on FIRS portal and get evidence of payment from bank, provide schedules of pension deduction to all PFAs.
	Manage audits for different state parastatals ITF, OYIRS, different arms of FIRS, LIRS, NSITF, NEPC etc.
	Respond to various official tax notices requesting information or providing clarifications.
	Liase with tax consultants and government officials on various issues on certificate on QCE, export, grant, Transfer pricing etc.
	Obtain employees&rsquo; annual TCC.
	Negotiation of fees with external consultants on all issues not limited to tax and audits.
	Ensure closures of all pending audits, make payment of any additional liabilities.
	Any other responsibility that may be assigned by the assigned supervisor or may come up during the month or year.


Competence &amp; Skills Required


	Education: Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field and must be a member of an accounting professional Institute.
	General Accounting knowledge.
	Analytical Background on VAT, WHT, PAYE, Pension, NSITF and CIT
	Strong technical skills to analyze business transactions and tax consequences.
	Must possess an in-depth understanding of complex tax laws.
	Stay up to date with new and changing tax policies.
	Accounting including IFRS, SAP and Local GAAP.
	Alignment skill.
	Finance background.
	Administration.
	Monthly planning and control cycle, efficient working of Accounts Payable, Receivables, Property, Plant &amp; Equipment and General Ledger and ensure compliance with relevant laws and regulations and application of relevant accounting standards.
	Analytical background, computer skills: MS Windows, including intermediate Excel and Word, and basic PowerPoint; SAP experience


Salary
N350,000 - N400,000 / month.

Method of Application&nbsp;

Bank and Tax Accountant Job at MarbleClear Limited




        
    

        
        MarbleClear Limited, a human Resource Consulting firm, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Bank and Tax Accountant

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
Bank Reconciliation:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Daily Transactions: Review and record daily bank transactions, including deposits, withdrawals, transfers, and journal entries.
	Reconciliation: Perform bank reconciliations, including matching bank charges and interest with internal records.
	Compliance: Ensure compliance with banking regulations and internal control policies.
	Preparing the documents required to request bank credit, following up on the credit activation after issuance and reviewing its balances (for letters of credit & letters of guarantee/ loans).
	Following-up long-term loan balances, debit interest, loan repayment installments, bank account reconciliations, and preparation of journal accounting entries for loans and accrued interest.
	Following up, controlling and reviewing bank charges and commissions and their conformity with the contracts concluded, analysis them and reporting to the direct manager
	review the bank charges and commissions that are deducted from the company's current account and must review their value according to the contract with the bank
	Initiate and Register e-Form M: Complete and register electronic Form M on the Nigeria Single Window Trade Portal for all importations
	Revalue open accounts payable (AP) and accounts receivable (AR) in foreign currencies at month-end.
	Calculate and record unrealized exchange gains or losses in the General Ledger.


Tax:


	Monthly provisions for minimum tax and additional PAYE
	Monthly compliance on VAT, WHT, PAYE, Pension, NSITF and Union and coop deductions ensure correct deductions and calculations have been inputted into the system.
	Review all calculations, prepare schedule for payment and payment instruction where applicable, file monthly returns on FIRS portal and get evidence of payment from bank, provide schedules of pension deduction to all PFAs.
	Manage audits for different state parastatals ITF, OYIRS, different arms of FIRS, LIRS, NSITF, NEPC etc.
	Respond to various official tax notices requesting information or providing clarifications.
	Liase with tax consultants and government officials on various issues on certificate on QCE, export, grant, Transfer pricing etc.
	Obtain employees’ annual TCC.
	Negotiation of fees with external consultants on all issues not limited to tax and audits.
	Ensure closures of all pending audits, make payment of any additional liabilities.
	Any other responsibility that may be assigned by the assigned supervisor or may come up during the month or year.


Competence & Skills Required


	Education: Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field and must be a member of an accounting professional Institute.
	General Accounting knowledge.
	Analytical Background on VAT, WHT, PAYE, Pension, NSITF and CIT
	Strong technical skills to analyze business transactions and tax consequences.
	Must possess an in-depth understanding of complex tax laws.
	Stay up to date with new and changing tax policies.
	Accounting including IFRS, SAP and Local GAAP.
	Alignment skill.
	Finance background.
	Administration.
	Monthly planning and control cycle, efficient working of Accounts Payable, Receivables, Property, Plant & Equipment and General Ledger and ensure compliance with relevant laws and regulations and application of relevant accounting standards.
	Analytical background, computer skills: MS Windows, including intermediate Excel and Word, and basic PowerPoint; SAP experience


Salary
N350,000 - N400,000 / month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should please send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as subject of the mail.
